name: Vanilla Extract
comments: FDA CFR Title 21 - 169.175 Vanilla Extract: "In vanilla extract the content of ethyl alcohol is not less than 35 percent by volume..."(https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/section-169.175). The lack of sugars is intentional; many similar products don't have 'Sugar' listed in the ingredients.

name: USDA Vanilla Extract
comments: USDA FoodData Central - SR Legacy - Vanilla extract (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/food-details/173471/nutrients).

name: USDA Imitation Vanilla Extract
comments: USDA FoodData Central - SR Legacy - Vanilla extract, imitation, alcohol (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/food-details/172235/nutrients). It lists 'Carbohydrate, by difference' as 2.41g, but no 'Total Sugars', which the other vanilla extract entries do list, so I didn't include any sugars here.

name: USDA No-Alcohol Imitation Vanilla Extract
comments: USDA FoodData Central - SR Legacy - Vanilla extract, imitation, no alcohol (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/food-details/172236/nutrients).

name: Lemon Zest
comments: USDA FoodData Central - SR Legacy - Lemon peel, raw (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/food-details/167749/nutrients). 'Total Sugars' is listed as 4.17g with no breakdown, so assume equal parts glucose, fructose, and sucrose.

name: Fresh Peppermint
comments: USDA FoodData Central - SR Legacy - Peppermint, fresh (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/food-details/173474/nutrients).

name: Fresh Spearmint
comments: USDA FoodData Central - SR Legacy - Spearmint, fresh (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/food-details/173475/nutrients).

name: Nielsen-Massey Pure Vanilla Extract
comments: Ingredients: Water, Alcohol (35%), Sugar, Vanilla Bean Extractives. Label: 15kcal, 0g fat, 0g sugar per 1 tsp (5ml). Sources: https://www.nielsen-massey.nl/consumer/products-pure-vanilla-extract.php, https://nielsenmassey.com/products/pure-vanilla-extract/. Sugar is present (3rd ingredient), but must be under 0.5g/serving since the label states 0g sugar, so it is capped at under 10% (0.5g/5ml). This doesn't quite reach the label's 15kcal/5ml.

name: Nielsen-Massey Pure Vanilla Bean Paste
comments: Ingredients: Sugar, Water, Vanilla Extract, Vanilla Beans, Gum Tragacanth (a natural thickener). 17kcal per 1 tsp (5ml) serving. "To replace vanilla extract in a recipe, simply measure the same amount of our Pure Vanilla Bean Paste and add. Or, replace one tablespoon of paste for every one whole vanilla bean." Sources: https://www.nielsen-massey.nl/consumer/products-pure-vanilla-bean-paste.php, https://nielsenmassey.com/products/pure-vanilla-bean-paste/. The sources state 9g of sugar per tbsp (~15ml), or 3g per tsp (5ml) on the nutrition facts table. With a sucrose density of 1.59g/ml that works out to ~50% sucrose by weight. Assuming the 'Vanilla Extract' used is 35% ABV, a ~50/25/25 breakdown of sucrose, water, and 'Vanilla Extract' works out to ~15kcal per 1 tsp (5ml), which is consistent with the label ordering but doesn't quite reach 17kcal. I tested a 1% Guar Gum solution and it looked about the same as the paste, but that was without 50% sugar, so using 0.5% Guar Gum (strength: 200) as a proxy for Gum Tragacanth.

name: Nielsen-Massey Pure Vanilla Powder
comments: Ingredients: Maltodextrin (a modified cornstarch), Vanilla Extract. 20kcal, 5g total carbohydrate, 0g sugar per 1 tsp (5ml). "...alcohol and sugar-free powder...". Sources: https://www.nielsen-massey.nl/consumer/products-pure-vanilla-powder.php, https://nielsenmassey.com/products/pure-vanilla-powder/. Stated alcohol- and sugar-free, so modeled as 100% Maltodextrin. 5g of carbohydrates at ~4kcal/g reproduces the 20kcal per serving - disregarding the powder's low bulk density and volume-based serving size.

name: Nielsen-Massey Pure Almond Extract
comments: Ingredients: Cane Alcohol (90%), Bitter Almond Oil, Water. 25kcal, 0g fat per 1 tsp (5ml). Sources: https://www.nielsen-massey.nl/consumer/products-pure-almond-extract.php, https://nielsenmassey.com/products/pure-almond-extract/. At 90% ABV the alcohol alone is ~24kcal/5ml; a little bitter almond oil (benzaldehyde), modeled as 5% fat (~0.2g/serving, rounds to the label's 0g), brings it to ~25kcal.

name: Nielsen-Massey Pure Chocolate Extract
comments: Ingredients: Water, Alcohol (37%), Cocoa Extract. 15kcal, 0g fat, 0g sugar per 1 tsp (5ml). "1 Tbs. Chocolate Extract = 1 Tbs. Unsweetened Cocoa Powder.". Sources: https://www.nielsen-massey.nl/consumer/products-pure-chocolate-extract.php, https://nielsenmassey.com/products/pure-chocolate-extract/. At 37% ABV the alcohol is ~10kcal/5ml; cocoa butter carried by the cocoa extract, modeled as 10% fat (~0.46g/serving, rounds to the label's 0g), brings it to ~15kcal.

name: Nielsen-Massey Pure Coffee Extract
comments: Ingredients: Water, Alcohol (35%), Coffee Extract. 20kcal, 0g fat, 0g carbohydrate per 1 tsp (5ml). "1 tsp. Coffee Extract = 1 tsp. Dry Espresso Powder.". Sources: https://www.nielsen-massey.nl/consumer/products-pure-coffee-extract.php, https://nielsenmassey.com/products/pure-coffee-extract/. At 35% ABV the alcohol is ~10kcal/5ml; coffee oils modeled as 10% fat (~0.47g/serving, rounds to the label's 0g) bring it to ~14kcal. The label's 20kcal is not reproducible at 35% ABV with all-zero macros (reaching 17.5kcal would need ~19% fat, ~0.9g/serving showing as 1g), so it appears rounded up.

name: Nielsen-Massey Pure Lemon Extract
comments: Ingredients: Cane Alcohol (90%), Lemon Oil, Water. 30kcal, 0g fat per 1 tsp (5ml). Sources: https://www.nielsen-massey.nl/consumer/products-pure-lemon-extract.php, https://nielsenmassey.com/products/pure-lemon-extract/. Lemon oil (d-limonene) is listed before water, so it is a notable fraction; modeled as 11% fat (~0.44g/serving, just under the 0.5g threshold so it rounds to the label's 0g). With 90% ABV alcohol (~24kcal) plus the oil this works out to ~28kcal/5ml (rounds to 30).

name: Nielsen-Massey Pure Orange Extract
comments: Ingredients: Cane Alcohol (90%), Orange Oil, Water. 30kcal, 0g fat per 1 tsp (5ml). Sources: https://www.nielsen-massey.nl/consumer/products-pure-orange-extract.php, https://nielsenmassey.com/products/pure-orange-extract/. Orange oil (d-limonene)is listed before water, so it is a notable fraction; modeled as 11% fat (~0.44g/serving, just under the 0.5g threshold so it rounds to the label's 0g). With 90% ABV alcohol (~24kcal) plus the oil this works out to ~28kcal/5ml (rounds to 30).

name: Nielsen-Massey Pure Peppermint Extract
comments: Ingredients: Cane Alcohol, Peppermint Oil (no water). 30kcal, 0g fat per 1 tsp (5ml). Sources: https://www.nielsen-massey.nl/consumer/products-pure-peppermint-extract.php, https://nielsenmassey.com/products/pure-peppermint-extract-2/. No water in the ingredients implies a high-proof base, so ABV is estimated at 90% (the label does not specify). Peppermint oil modeled as 11% fat (~0.44g/serving, rounds to the label's 0g); with the alcohol this works out to ~28kcal/5ml (rounds to 30).

name: Angostura Aromatic Bitters
comments: Ingredients: Water, alcohol, spices, natural aromas, sugar, colorant: Caramel E150a. Source: https://angosturabitters.com/portfolio/aromatic-bitters/. 3% sugars is a guess; it should be very little since it's listed after the spices and aromas.

name: Angostura Orange Bitters
comments: Ingredients: Water, glycerine, alcohol, natural flavors, beta-carotene (color). Source: https://angosturabitters.com/portfolio/orange-bitters/

name: Dillon's Orange Bitters
comments: Ingredients: Alcohol, Water, Sugar, Orange peel, Natural flavour, Spices. Source: https://shop.dillons.ca/orange-bitters.html. 10% sugars is a guess, in line with similar products that list 'Sugar' as the 3rd ingredient.

name: Essential Oil
comments: This is a generic entry for pure essential oils, which are highly concentrated aromatic extracts derived from plants. They are typically composed of volatile compounds like terpenes, terpenoids, phenols, aldehydes, esters, etc. It's not currently possible to model these complex compositions in this library, but essential oils are used in such minute quantities that their composition is negligible in the formulation.
